patterns.name: Duplicated ConnectOptions construction in pybind client constructors
patterns.rationale: The py::init lambdas for the KV, Object and Hetero pybind clients each hand-build a ConnectOptions struct from the same host/port/timeout/credential/tenant constructor arguments using an identical designated-initializer sequence. The Object and Hetero blocks are byte-for-byte identical except for the trailing enableCrossNodeConnection/enableRemoteH2D fields. This copy-paste means any change to ConnectOptions field wiring (new field, renamed field, default change) must be repeated across every pybind_register_*.cpp, and it is easy to silently diverge (e.g. Object hardcodes enableCrossNodeConnection = false while Hetero/KV thread it through). A shared helper, e.g. MakeConnectOptions(...), would remove the repetition and keep the mapping consistent.

locations.existing_code:
ConnectOptions connectOpts{
                .host = host,
                .port = port,
                .connectTimeoutMs = connectTimeoutMs,
                .requestTimeoutMs = reqTimeoutMs,
                .token = token,
                .clientPublicKey = clientPublicKey,
                .clientPrivateKey = clientPrivateKey,
                .serverPublicKey = serverPublicKey,
                .accessKey = accessKey,
                .secretKey = secretKey,
                .tenantId = tenantId,
                .enableCrossNodeConnection = false,
                .enableExclusiveConnection = enableExclusiveConnection,
            };

ConnectOptions connectOpts{
                .host = host,
                .port = port,
                .connectTimeoutMs = connectTimeoutMs,
                .requestTimeoutMs = reqTimeoutMs,
                .token = token,
                .clientPublicKey = clientPublicKey,
                .clientPrivateKey = clientPrivateKey,
                .serverPublicKey = serverPublicKey,
                .accessKey = accessKey,
                .secretKey = secretKey,
                .tenantId = tenantId,
                .enableCrossNodeConnection = enableCrossNodeConnection,
                .enableExclusiveConnection = enableExclusiveConnection,
                .enableRemoteH2D = enableRemoteH2D
            };

ConnectOptions connectOpts{ .host = host,
                                        .port = port,
                                        .connectTimeoutMs = connectTimeoutMs,
                                        .requestTimeoutMs = reqTimeoutMs,

                                        .token = token,
                                        .clientPublicKey = clientPublicKey,
                                        .clientPrivateKey = clientPrivateKey,
                                        .serverPublicKey = serverPublicKey,
                                        .accessKey = accessKey,
                                        .secretKey = secretKey,
                                        .tenantId = tenantId,
                                        .enableCrossNodeConnection = enableCrossNodeConnection,
                                        .enableExclusiveConnection = enableExclusiveConnection,
                                        .fastTransportMemSize = fastTransportMemSize };

patterns.name: Duplicated "init" binding lambda (TraceGuard + client.Init())
patterns.rationale: The Object and Stream pybind registrations expose an "init" method with a byte-for-byte identical lambda body: set a request-scoped TraceGuard then return client.Init(). The Hetero variant is the same plus a py::gil_scoped_release, and the Perf variant is the same with SetTraceUUID instead of SetRequestTraceUUID. This TraceGuard-then-delegate shape is repeated for essentially every bound method in these files; a small wrapper (e.g. a traced() helper that installs the TraceGuard and forwards to the member function) would collapse the boilerplate and guarantee every entry point is traced consistently.
